channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government decides to control further fuel price hikes amid global oil surge
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The federal government has imposed a ban on the export of all petroleum products and is contemplating not increasing domestic fuel prices further despite a steady rise in global oil rates. ...

item.title: The Importance of Emotional Equality in Healthy Relationships
item.description: This article talk about how emotional equality dey very important for any good relationship. E no be about who give more love or do big big things, but about both partners dey give fully and equally. ...

item.title: Government Officials Responsible for Majority of Social Grant Fraud, Warns IEJ
item.description: A recent analysis by the Institute for Economic Justice (IEJ) reveals that between 2014 and 2025, roughly 75% of social grant fraud cases in South Africa involved government employees, contractors or ...

item.title: Iran’s Leader Highlights Strategic Importance of Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader has publicly stated that the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global shipping route for oil, should be leveraged amid ongoing regional tensions. This marks his first official statement ...

item.title: Farooq Abdullah Survives Attempt on Life, Demands Comprehensive Investigation
item.description: On March 11, 2026, former Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister and National Conference president Farooq Abdullah, aged 88, narrowly escaped an assassination attempt during a wedding function in Jammu. A l...

item.title: Werder Bremen Defender Felix Agu Returns to Full Team Training After Adductor Injury
item.description: Werder Bremen don receive better news as dem Nigerian defender, Felix Agu, don resume full team training on Thursday ahead of dia Bundesliga match against FSV Mainz on Sunday. The 26-year-old player b...

item.title: Guide to Philippine Government Support for OFWs During Middle East Conflict
item.description: As tensions in the Middle East rise following military strikes involving the United States and Israel, more Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) are seeking help, including repatriation. The Philippine go...

item.title: Northern Youths Condemn Sowore Over Comments on Defence Minister Musa
item.description: On Thursday, March 12, 2026, one northern youth organization wey dem call Northern Nigeria United Youths for Peace and Progress, come out strongly against Omoyele Sowore, the activist and former presi...

item.title: White House Faces Criticism for AI Video Depicting Iran Conflict Using Bowling Imagery
item.description: The Trump White House faced widespread backlash online after posting a controversial AI-generated video related to the ongoing conflict with Iran. The 34-second clip begins with footage of U.S. bowlin...

item.title: Centre proposes rationalising virus testing for plasma-based medicines
item.description: The Indian government has put forward a proposal to amend drug regulations, aiming to eliminate the requirement of repeat virus tests on medicines produced from human plasma. These medicines, includin...

item.title: Tanzania and Uganda start talks to boost cooperation in politics, trade, and agriculture
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania and Uganda have started official bilateral talks to improve cooperation and boost joint development in the East African region. The discussions, which began on March 11 and wi...

item.title: Jonah Pezet leads Eels to 40-32 comeback victory over Broncos
item.description: Parramatta playmaker Jonah Pezet was the standout performer in a thrilling NRL clash against Brisbane, securing a 40-32 win in a match that swung back and forth. The Broncos initially looked dominant ...

item.title: Local Teachers’ Union Criticizes $750 Supply Cards as Insufficient
item.description: The Elementary Teachers’ Federation of Ontario (ETFO) in Windsor has voiced strong criticism of the provincial government's recent initiative to provide $750 supply cards to teachers. Local ETFO Presi...

item.title: Iran's New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Vows Continued Revenge and Strait of Hormuz Blockade Amid Escalating War
item.description: The summary above has approximately 320 words.

item.title: Cabinet minister defends Keir Starmer's response to Lord Mandelson's controversial US ambassador appointment amid Epstein links revelations
item.description: A senior Cabinet Office minister, Nick Thomas-Symonds, has defended Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's handling of Lord Peter Mandelson's appointment as UK ambassador to the United States, despite admi...

item.title: Study Reveals Cellular Energy Imbalance in Young Adults with Major Depressive Disorder
item.description: Researchers from the University of Queensland, in collaboration with the University of Minnesota, have identified a novel cellular mechanism potentially underlying major depressive disorder (MDD) in y...

item.title: Iran and Israel conflict intensifies, impacting oil markets and regional security
item.description: The conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran has sharply escalated, causing significant disruption to energy markets across the Middle East. Iran reportedly set two tankers ablaze in Iraqi waters, wh...

item.title: Weekend Guide: Activities for Shopping, Celebrating, and Enjoying Outdoors

item.title: Survivor 50 Episode 3 Recap: Fan-Driven Tribe Swap Causes Chaos
item.description: In the third episode of Survivor 50, an all-star, fan-influenced season, pre-season fan votes continued to shake up the game. Contestants were instructed to 'drop their buffs,' resulting in a reshuffl...

item.title: Johannesburg residents report sulphur odour linked to hydrogen sulphide levels
item.description: Residents across Johannesburg, from Sandton to Roodepoort and Midrand, have recently reported a strong sulphur-like odour reminiscent of rotten eggs. The unusual smell, first noticed in January and re...
